signal-town-cold-signal-open: Outside, the mailbox flag snaps up. Inside is a photograph of someone wearing Bosch's face and walking uphill before sunrise.
Bosch was asleep when it was taken. On the back, MiFella wrote: I sent it to the arcade kids. They put a milady on the other you. Everybody gets the joke but us.
MiFella saw the masked figure near the meteor. Follow him uphill and find out what he photographed.
onett-brother-fallsin: MiFella catches up beside the meteor, holding his phone too tightly.
"I only sent the picture to a few people. They put the milady on him and sent it around. Every time it came back, more people on the floor said the masked guy was you."
"They called it onboarding. Cute, right? Also bad. Probably a rug. Check the wreckage. I will watch the road."

signal-town-card-clique: CARD CLIQUE: "gm, Bosch. We put your face on the milady. Everybody on the floor gets the joke now. Well, almost everybody."
CARD CLIQUE: "Don't look so worried. Put one on, pay the dues, and we'll explain the bit after you're in it."
MiFella: "Bosch! There you are. See? I told them the mask would look good on you."
THE NEAREST SCREEN, using Bosch's voice a moment late: "There you are."
signal-town-card-clique-reveal: MiFella: "Whoa, the floor sent us an onboarding pass. I didn't even ask. That's pretty cool."
MiFella: "They want us at the civic gate. They said I have a good eye for recruits. That sounds good, right?"
MiFella: "It's east, then uphill, by THE PRECINCT. C'mon, let's go before they change their minds!"
relay-gate-returnless-king: RETURNLESS KING: "Pass accepted. Welcome back. ...Wait. Bosch joined ten minutes ago. He got the joke. So who are you."
RETURNLESS KING: "The Bosch on the floor is onboarded and on-message. You still need a private interview."
RETURNLESS KING: "...That is a bent card, not a member badge. Waving it harder does not help."
north-route-gate-warning: ROUTE AIDE, not looking up: "Onboarded Bosch, cleared. Cleared. ...Oh. You are the one without a mask. Sorry, today's road is members only."
ROUTE AIDE: "The Bosch who joined is already through. The Bosch outside the bit has to wait."
first-threshold-malady: MASKED BOSCH: "MiFella gave the group your traits. You hate tomatoes, sleep through alarms, and act like you don't care when people leave. That's enough to put on the milady."
MASKED BOSCH: "...What is that in your hand. A little ceramic heart. There is a chamber in it and it holds nothing."
MASKED BOSCH: "MiFella gave you that. The onboarding notes do not have the heart. Why don't they have the heart."
MASKED BOSCH: "MiFella says you hate tomatoes. MiFella says you sleep late. The floor says I am Bosch."
first-threshold-malady-reveal: MiFella: "The mask is falling apart. Those are my photos. My route stamps. That's everything I gave them, spilling out."
MiFella: "They said the more I put in, the safer we'd be. So I gave them money, pictures, all of it."
MiFella's phone buzzes. He turns it face-down. "...Forget you saw that. Come on."
Bosch does not follow. The ceramic heart is still in his hand.
BOSCH: "You told them I act like I don't care when people leave."
BOSCH: "That one was true. You knew it was true. You gave it to them anyway."
MiFella starts to say something and stops. For once he does not have the bit ready.
Then he turns and walks to the onboarding car idling past the gate. A smiling attendant waves him through.
MiFella gets in without looking back. Bosch does not follow. The road north runs on toward Postwick, one friend short.
recruit-munch: Munch steps out from behind the hotel sign. His pockets are full of rejected receipts.
MUNCH: "That recruit knew the traits people posted about you. It didn't know what your friends remember. I can spot the holes in their bit."
Munch joins the crew. He can SPY on enemies to reveal their strengths and weaknesses.
leave-signal-town: The north gate is quiet now. The onboarding car MiFella left in is already gone.
Bosch keeps the damaged Swag Deck. Unfiled, unflattering, his.
The route the group laid out runs on toward Postwick.
postwick-arrival: Postwick welcomes Bosch with a giant picture of MiFella in a Remilia Co. onboarding uniform.
Shopkeepers, crossing guards, and kids on the sidewalk all say gm the same way. One resident stops in the middle and quietly asks Bosch for help.
Remilia Co. promises instant friends for one easy payment. Its Registry picks which traits go under each mask.
Find the Registry near the town records. Learn what MiFella paid and what he gave away.
recruit-cloak: Cloak steps out of the camera's blind spot. The greeting plays, but Cloak doesn't say it.
CLOAK: "Remilia Co. recruited my neighbors one sentence at a time. Help them remember something the group didn't give them."
Cloak joins the crew. Cloak's PSI can protect people and break through hostile signals.
postwick-registry: The Registry has a file called BOSCH, CARE INSTRUCTIONS. MiFella's name is beside every private detail.
REGISTRY WARDEN: "He paid his onboarding fee and gave us the hard parts. Our Bosch never feels lonely, embarrassed, or left out of the joke."
Bosch knows the facts in the file. He doesn't know the person they made from them.
The Warden lifts a laminated smile. Bosch fights to keep MiFella's mistake from becoming the rule for all of Postwick.
postwick-registry-reveal: The Warden drops a Remilia Co. ledger. Every name has a fee beside it and one feeling the recruit wanted gone.
Several residents start the same slogan, stop, and try again in their own voices.
MiFella's file went to THE VENUE, where Remilia Co. turns new members into entertainment for the floor.
Follow the bright signs and track the file through the arena.
arena-venue-1: THE VENUE hangs MiFella's onboarding portrait above the ring. The crowd claps before Bosch even walks in.
ANNOUNCER: "Tonight's champion wins the feeling the whole floor already picked!"
Someone near the ring forgets to clap. Another person notices. A hole opens in the bit.
The first floor enforcer rushes forward to close it.
arena-venue-2: The second bracket begins while the crowd tries to find the beat again.
A kid shouts the wrong slogan and laughs. Three nearby adults drop the bit and laugh too.
ANNOUNCER: "That feeling was not approved. Please enjoy onboarding."
arena-venue-3: The final bracket lights up. The Venue tells the crowd that everybody knows Bosch will lose.
Cloak hears people talking under the chant. "Don't hit the chorus. Pray, and give them room to hear each other."
The Soul Consuming Flame feeds on everyone staying in the bit. Every real voice makes it smaller.
postwick-act2-end: The Venue plays MiFella's whole intake recording. He looks tired and relieved. He even smiles when Remilia Co. says his attention has value.
MIFELLA: "I gave them the private stuff and the money. They said the milady could carry the parts that hurt. I thought I was helping."
He hears Bosch say his name and looks away. "They send anything that doesn't fit to Dead Letter. If you want the parts I gave away, start there."
MiFella ends the call before Bosch can forgive him or blame him. The road to Dead Letter opens.
deadletter-arrival: Dead Letter keeps everything Remilia Co. threw out for being messy, embarrassing, or hard to explain.
People compare returned photos. Their memories don't match, but they still belong to real people.
MiFella's rejected onboarding papers lead to the Museum of Leaked Versions. Something inside has been feeding lines to every local group.
Recover the undelivered SOURCE, then enter the museum.
museum-starman: The museum guardian asks each party member for the one story about Bosch that the group should keep.
Munch shares what he noticed. Cloak shares a secret. Bosch's family remembers the crooked picture he always stops to fix.
The stories don't match, but none is a lie. The guardian can't squeeze them all under one mask.
museum-frank: A polished MiFella steps down from the exhibit. He has the milady on and says every trait he handed over during onboarding. He sounds like he's reading someone else's homework.
Bosch asks about that last afternoon in his room. The mask knows the date, the photo, every trait MiFella handed over.
It doesn't know MiFella dug that heart out of a gacha machine and laughed like it was the funniest thing, just to watch Bosch keep it. He never told the group that part.
For one second, the real MiFella looks out from under the milady. "The heart. The ceramic one, that holds nothing. It's in your pocket right now." The mask slips.
museum-worm: Deep in the vault, a knot of labels explains every little thing until there is no person left underneath.
PROVENANCE 0: "One approved story can settle this. Put on the milady. Let the group finish onboarding MiFella for you."
Bosch refuses. Caring about someone doesn't mean pretending they did nothing wrong. It doesn't mean throwing them away, either.
Munch says what happened. Cloak guards what stayed private. Bosch talks to MiFella like he can still choose what to do next.
deadletter-act3-end: The museum's labels go quiet. But the same joke is still in every store, school, and home. Nobody will explain it unless you join.
The records call it Milady. It isn't one boss hiding behind the screens. It's the cult that makes the floor feel easier than thinking for yourself.
MiFella sends one message without a script: "The cake was Strawberry because you hated the frosting. I remember. Please come home."
Far away, every light in Morningside turns on at once. Every screen says gm.
endgame-return: Milady's local group has taken over Morningside. Familiar people wear the mask at breakfast, at work, and on the walk home.
People Bosch helped in Postwick and Dead Letter call into town. Their rough, uneven voices give the recruits something else to hear.
Three cells hold the local floor together. Reach the people inside each one. They are recruits, not disposable enemies.
raid-morningside-1: The first cell is made of shopkeepers Bosch knows. They ring up dues beside the milk and repeat a Remilia Co. checkout line.
A Postwick resident calls one shopkeeper by an old nickname and remembers a kindness that cost nothing. The shopkeeper misses the next line.
The cell weakens when one person recognizes another. The rest of the group moves in.
raid-morningside-2: The second cell recruited Bosch's family at breakfast. Each person smiles over the same empty plate.
Cloak protects the family's private memories. Munch points out the gaps between the masks and the people he met.
Their own voices come back under the chant. The group holding the room together drops the bit.
raid-morningside-3: The final cell turns on every screen in town. MiFella appears in his onboarding uniform, then takes off the badge.

MIFELLA: "I took the first picture and shared it. I gave Remilia Co. Bosch's private stuff and my money after he told me to stop. Those masked recruits were not Bosch. Getting into the group did not make what I did okay."
MIFELLA: "You remember the bank? We were gonna pull it all out and just bolt. Solana Beach, the Galleria, anywhere. Together. I spent mine getting onboarded instead."
He sends the truth over the same lines that carried the lie. Recruits stop repeating the bit and answer in their own voices.
With nobody left on-message, the local milady can barely hold its shape.
milady-final: In the empty plaza, the local milady tries on every easy mask of Bosch. None of them fits.
Bosch pulls MiFella's old hoodie tighter. It is wrecked and it is his, and the clean versions never once got that part right.
MILADY: "I am the bit under the floor, the market, and the record. Join the group. Be our exit liquidity. You never have to be the only anon who doesn't get the joke again."
Bosch hears MiFella, his family, and everyone he helped answer in their own voices. Nobody has the whole story. Everybody stays.
Pray to keep the channel open. Protect the people speaking. Fight only the mask left behind when they leave the group.
endgame-finale: The local milady falls apart when the last on-message voice chooses a person over the group.
Morningside doesn't settle on one approved story. People compare memories, disagree, apologize, and keep talking.
MiFella says he's sorry without asking Bosch to forget or forgive him right away. Bosch leaves the conversation open.
The Remilia Co. signs go dark. Milady can still show up wherever people pay for belonging and stop thinking for themselves. But tonight, the rug stops here.
source-vacancy: The motel strip is dead except for one buzzing marquee. It spells Bosch's thought back at him, then changes one word while the thought is still warm.
THE EDITOR: "Onboarding is a mercy. I cut the word that keeps you outside the group and leave a cleaner member standing."
The sign flickers. Bosch keeps the wrong word because it was his.
correction-editor-rematch: The marquee coughs up a second draft.
THE EDITOR: "A member can always be re-onboarded. That is the courtesy your originals keep refusing me."
The battle log is already changing its verbs.

objectiveTextById.act1-card-clique: Go west to MONS LINK and find out why the arcade put the milady on MiFella's photograph.
objectiveTextById.act1-returnless-king: Take the onboarding pass east, then uphill to the civic gate beside THE PRECINCT.
objectiveTextById.act1-malady: Follow the opened road to the hotel crossing and confront the Bosch wearing the milady.
objectiveTextById.act1-munch: Meet Munch beyond the hotel crossing and ask what the mask could not know.
objectiveTextById.act1-leave-signal-town: Continue to Morningside's exit and stop MiFella from joining the group.
objectiveTextById.act2-reach-postwick: Follow MiFella and the Remilia Co. recruitment route to Postwick.
objectiveTextById.act2-postwick-registry: Find Postwick's Registry near the town records and learn what MiFella paid and handed over.
objectiveTextById.act2-arena-venue-1: Follow MiFella's onboarding file to THE VENUE and enter the first exhibition bracket.
objectiveTextById.act2-arena-venue-2: Continue through THE VENUE while the audience starts dropping the bit.
objectiveTextById.act2-arena-venue-3: Finish THE VENUE exhibition. Pray so the crowd can speak in its own voices.
objectiveTextById.act2-leave-postwick: Play MiFella's intake recording at THE VENUE and follow the group's rejected records toward Dead Letter.
objectiveTextById.act2-source-spring: Attest the Spring SOURCE beyond Postwick, then continue toward Dead Letter.
objectiveTextById.act3-reach-dead-letter: Reach Dead Letter and find the records Remilia Co. cast out.
objectiveTextById.act3-source-undelivered: Recover the Undelivered SOURCE from Dead Letter's return yard.
objectiveTextById.act3-museum-starman: Enter the Museum of Leaked Versions and answer the group's demand for one approved Bosch.
objectiveTextById.act3-museum-frank: Use a private memory to reach MiFella under the polished milady.
objectiveTextById.act3-museum-worm: Confront Provenance 0 in the deep vault without letting the group finish onboarding MiFella.
objectiveTextById.endgame-return: Return to occupied Morningside and reconnect with the recruits inside Milady's three local cells.
objectiveTextById.raid-morningside-1: Reach the outer block and help its shopkeepers drop the bit.
objectiveTextById.raid-morningside-2: Push toward Bosch's home and reconnect with his family inside the second cell.
objectiveTextById.raid-morningside-3: Reach the town screens and give MiFella room to tell the truth about the first picture.
objectiveTextById.milady-final: Approach the weakened local milady in the plaza. Pray, protect the voices, and fight the mask the recruits leave behind.

act2-postwick-registry: The Registry is beside Postwick's town records. MiFella's onboarding file is inside.
Follow the residents repeating the same welcome. The Registry installed their script.
act2-arena-venue-1: MiFella's file was transferred to THE VENUE. Follow the exhibition lights.
The Registry ledger points to THE VENUE, where Postwick rehearses its happiness.
act2-arena-venue-2: Stay inside THE VENUE. The second bracket begins where the crowd loses its rhythm.
act2-arena-venue-3: The final bracket feeds on agreement. Pray so the audience can hear individual voices.
act2-leave-postwick: The champion file at THE VENUE contains MiFella's real intake recording.
Play MiFella's message, then follow the rejected records toward Dead Letter.
act3-reach-dead-letter: Dead Letter keeps the records Remilia Co. rejected. Follow the return route into town.
act3-source-undelivered: The Undelivered source is a wet parcel in Dead Letter's return yard.
act3-museum-starman: Bring the recovered contradiction to the Museum of Leaked Versions lobby.
act3-museum-frank: A polished MiFella manifestation is deeper in the museum. Ask about the collapsed birthday cake.
act3-museum-worm: Provenance 0 is in the deep vault. Keep speaking to MiFella as someone who can still choose.
endgame-return: The recovered records and MiFella's message point home. Return to Morningside.
Milady has a local foothold in Morningside, but the people inside it can still disconnect.
raid-morningside-1: Start at Morningside's outer shops. Call the synchronized residents by what you remember about them.
raid-morningside-2: The second cell is pushing toward Bosch's house. Protect the household's private memories.
raid-morningside-3: The final cell controls the town screens. Reach MiFella and let him correct the first account.
milady-final: The local manifestation is exposed in Morningside's plaza. Pray to keep the witness channel open.
Protect the distinct voices. Fight the local shape that remains after people disconnect.

pages: "That flash came from Bosch's window. MiFella was outside taking pictures instead of running."
"He cares about you. He also loves being the first person to find something strange. Keep those two facts together."
"Someone with your face crossed the yard before sunrise. You were still asleep."
"MiFella followed it uphill. He looked frightened until his phone started filling with replies."
"The other Bosch walked past every family picture without looking. You always stop at the crooked one."
"MiFella went north toward the meteor. Find him before the crowd decides what he saw."
The house phone rings once, then stops.
MiFella left a message: "I saw the other Bosch go uphill. I posted one picture. Please do not be mad until you see it."
MiFella pounds on the door.
"Bosch, get up. I saw another you outside while you were still asleep."
"I took a picture and sent it to the arcade group. They keep sending back cleaner versions. They call it getting you onboarded."
"The other you went uphill toward the meteor. Get dressed and meet me outside. We need to see what I started."
A terminal wakes inside the wreckage. Beside it, blue light gathers into Bosch's outline.
LOG: FIRST ONBOARDED BOSCH. SOURCE IMAGE CIRCULATING.
The onboarded copy watches Bosch as if waiting for instructions. It can repeat an action one turn late, but it cannot understand why Bosch chose it.
MiFella whispers: "That is the version they sent back to me."
"MiFella called. The onboarded Bosch at the meteor came from his photograph."
"The arcade group is still circulating it. MONS LINK is west along the town road."
"The onboarded copy could repeat what you did. It could not explain why."
"Take MiFella to the arcade. He needs to see what sharing that picture changed."
"MiFella is pretending the milady stunt was only funny because he feels guilty."
"Stay kind and stay honest. MONS LINK is west on the main road."
The house phone rings.
MiFella left a message: "The arcade kids are making more onboarded Boschs. I am going back to ask them to stop. Probably."
The vendor lowers a stack of identical bootlegs. "The Registry cracked and I remembered I hate this cover."
"MiFella's file went to THE VENUE. Follow the exhibition lights and ask who profits from all that agreement."
The vendor repeats Postwick's welcome, then winces. "Sorry. That sentence keeps starting without me."
"The Registry near the town records installed it. MiFella went in smiling."
The bus driver kills the prerecorded applause. "MiFella left a real message in the champion file. Play it before you leave."
The tour bus idles beside THE VENUE. "That crowd practices feeling together until nobody notices who stopped."
"MiFella's intake file is in the bracket. Win slowly enough to listen."
The resident speaks without checking the museum wall. "Milady was the pressure making every label sound inevitable. Not a person. A habit with infrastructure."
"MiFella remembered something the network never received. Follow that voice home."
The resident holds two conflicting return slips. "Both are mine. That used to scare the museum."
"Recover the Undelivered source in the return yard, then bring the contradiction to the museum."
The dog sniffs the recovered parcel and chooses the damaged address over three clean copies.
Someone has written MUSEUM on the back in MiFella's handwriting.
The dog paws at the return yard, then looks toward the wet parcel nobody improved.
The Undelivered source is still there because nobody agreed on what it should become.
"I remembered three different versions of yesterday. Turns out I was tired in all of them."
"MiFella is helping people correct the records. Nobody has to forgive him to tell the truth beside him."
The resident's smile resets twice. "Find something I did that the script would never choose. Say it back to me."
The occupation cell tightens whenever the resident speaks in Milady's rhythm.
